task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: 11 Remote software upgrade via On Remote Service (ORS)

abstract.p: This document describes the upgrade procedure by using On Remote Service (ORS).
Remote software upgrade via ORS can be used to upgrade a machine without a service technician being on-site and without handling the files containing the upgrade.

li.p: When a new version is available for upgrade, the new upgrade package is made available at the back-office. A release process is in place that allows the NSO (National Service Organization) to rollout in a controlled way.
The software is then published to the machine.

Next, the upgrade package can be remotely downloaded on the printer through the ORS connection. (So no need to download manually the package up-front). The automated start of downloading depends on the setting “Download moment” in the “Configure software updates” in the Settings Editor.
Last step is to install the downloaded package on the machine.

abstract.p: The workflow related to ORS software updates is described here:
See: Upgrade workflows for ORS updates .

li.p: Modifications are not set when using ORS for software upgrade.
An ADAM entry is not created when using ORS for software upgrade.

note.p: A training video is available in the training video section: See Install firmware upgrades with On Remote Service (Service version)

li.p: Product Specialist rights within PRISMAservice. (Typical is S&S User role) This is necessary to announce the new software version to the machine. If you do not have these rights, request access or contact your regional support office.
Customer is informed on the system being connected to ORS.
In case additional actions are required to be executed by the customer, the customer needs to be well informed on the (timing of the) actions.

section.title: 1. The engine needs to be configured for ORS software updates
section.p: See: Configure, download, and install software updates from On Remote Service .

section.title: 2. The software needs to published to the engine
section.p: The software update publication to the engine is done in the service portal (https://prismaservice.cpp.canon). Publication in the service portal is done by opening the product by selecting the serial number, going to the “Software version” sub menu, and click the publish button.

entry.p: Configurations in the ORS Serviceportal (https://prismaservice.cpp.canon)
Firmware publishing
Follow central update policy.
When published by HQ the update will be published to the engine on the next ORS reconnect( daily around midnight)
No publication to engine
When HQ publishes the update this is reported here and not published to the engine until the publish button is pushed.
Windows update/Security publishing
When the windows updates are published by HQ the update will be published to the engine on the next ORS reconnect( daily around midnight). The windows update publishing can be forced.
Revoke the published software to the engine.

As long as the updates are not installed at the engine the updates offered to the engine can be revoked. The publishing settings for firmware and Windows updates as described above still apply. E.g. Not applied security updates will be published again to the engine on a reconnect.

section.title: 3. when the configuration on the engine for download and installation moment is set to manual

entry.p: Step
Action
Open the Settings Editor of the machine (http://<hostname_or_ip_address>).
Click on the “Support” tab and then click on “Software Updates”.
In "Available PRISMAsync and operating system updates from On Remote Service", you will see a line beginning containing a software version.

note.p: This can appear several minutes after having published the version in the back-office.

entry.p: Check the checkbox in front of the line and click on "Download" icon just above, the download process will start.
Depending on the configuration of the updates, the download can also start automatically.
The download time takes approximately 25 minutes. It depends on the band width of the Internet to the printer.
The "Progress" field will increase until 100% followed by a '-'.
The " Install" button is now available.
Click on “Install” and confirm with OK.
The verification of the update starts.

note.p: Do not close the browser during this phase.
If you relaunch the browser, you will not be able to know when the current progress will be at 100%, you'll just have to wait (approximately 25 minutes) before rebooting the system.

entry.p: After approximately 40 minutes, the current progress will be 100%

note.p: In case the session to the settings editor is lost, an error icon is shown without text. Respect the required time indicated and then restart the engine to continue. This can be done via the Engine Operator panel or via the Settings Editor ->Support-> troubleshooting-> restart engine.

entry.p: Click on “OK”, then you are invited to restart.
Verify there is no media on the platen, then select “Restart now” and click “OK”
The printer restarts.

note.p: If restart does not occur from having pressed "Restart now" in the Settings Editor window, then restart the printer via the printer user interface.
On the next start of the engine the installation process will start.

entry.p: After Restart, the user interface will display the installation wheel at step 19%.
Wait until the installation process is completed (the printer restarts several times).
After approximately 45 minutes the installation process is completed.
Confirm the software was upgraded as expected:

li.p: On the user panel: System tab > About > Version of printing system
In Settings Editor: Support tab > About > Version of printer software
